english: rgyu-ʼbrás (rgyu 'bras) cause and effect or consequence, gen. in a moral sense: actions and their fruits (lás-kyi rgyu-ʼbrás (las kyi rgyu 'bras)); also the doctrine treating on this subject, the doctrine of retribution, the principal dogma of Buddhism, prop.: las-rgyu-ʼbrás-kyi čos (las rgyu 'bras kyi chos); las-rgyu-ʼbrás-la yid-čes-pa (las rgyu 'bras la yid ches pa) to believe in the doctrine of retribution Glr. — rgyu-mtsán (rgyu mtshan) (Ssk. निमित्त) 1. cause, rgyu-mtsán odrí-ba (rgyu mtshan 'dri ba) to ask after the cause Glr.; rgyu-mtsán lo-rgyús odrí-ba (rgyu mtshan lo rgyus 'dri ba) to question closely, to examine rigorously Mil.; the connection of events, the manner in which a thing has come to pass, ṅaí naṅ-nas ṗye kyéŕ-bai rgyu-mtsán šod (nga'i nang nas phye khyer ba'i rgyu mtshan shod) tell me how it was that you could fetch the flour from my house, how you were able to accomplish it Mil. nt. 2. token, sign, characteristic, proof, evidence, odúg-pai rgyu-mtsán ('dug pa'i rgyu mtshan) as an evidence of being... Glr. II. instead of rgyú-ma (rgyu ma).

to go, walk, move, wander, range, of men, animals, and fig. of lifeless things, čú-la rgyú-bai odab-čágs (chu la rgyu ba'i 'dab chags) birds frequenting the water; kún-tu rgyú-ba (kun tu rgyu ba) to wander from one place to another, hence: kun-tu-rgyú (kun tu rgyu) itinerant monk, n. of a sect of the Brahmans Dzl.; rluṅ rgyú-bai rtsa (rlung rgyu ba'i rtsa) those veins in which air is circulating, cf. rtsa (rtsa) and rluṅ (rlung); also c. accus.: yul (yul), or groṅ rgyú-ba (grong rgyu ba) to rove through countries, through villages; rgyu-sráṅ (rgyu srang), btsán-gyi rgyu-sráṅ (btsan gyi rgyu srang) the road that is frequented by the btsan (btsan) (a kind of demons). — rgyu-skár (rgyu skar) v. below.

1. entrails, intestines, bowels, esp. the small intestines, opp. to loṅ-ka (long ka) the large intestines; rgyu k̥ril-ba (rgyu khril ba) convolvulus intestinorum Sch. (?); rgyu k̥róg-pa (rgyu khrog pa) the croaking of the bowels Sch.; rgyu-sgróg (rgyu sgrog) the caul, covering the lower intestines; rgyu-stód (rgyu stod) the upper bowels, rgyu-smád (rgyu smad) the lower bowels Cs.; rgyu-nád (rgyu nad) disease of the bowels; rgyu-gzér (rgyu gzer) colic. — 2. sausage, *gyú-ma gyáṅ-wa* (*gyu ma gyang wa*) C. (v. sgyóṅ-ba (sgyong ba)), *káṅ-če* (*kang che*) W. to stuff sausages; *gyú-ma kar-gyáṅ* (*gyu ma kar gyang*) meat-sausage, meat-pudding, *gyú-ma naq-gyáṅ* (*gyu ma nag gyang*) black-pudding C.

the lunar mansions, Ssk. नक्षत्र, or those 'constellations' through which the moon 'passes' in her revolution round the heavens; acc. to Wdk. and others they are the following: ༡ tha-skar (tha skar) (also dbyug-gu (dbyug gu)) three stars in the Ram's head; ༢ bra-nye (bra nye) (conceived by the Brahmans to be the image of the yoni); ༣ smin-drug (smin drug), the Pleiades; ༤ be-rdzi (be rdzi), snar-ma (snar ma); ༥ mgo (mgo), smal-po (smal po); ༦ lag (lag); ༧ nabs-so (nabs so), rgyal-stod (rgyal stod), nam-so (nam so); ༨ rgyal-smad (rgyal smad); ༩ skag (skag), wa (wa); ༡༠ mču (mchu), rta-pa (rta pa), rta-čen (rta chen) (with Regulus its brightest star); ༡༡ gre (gre), rtau (rta'u), rta-čuṅ (rta chung); ༡༢ dbo (dbo), k̥ra (khra); ༡༣ me-bži (me bzhi), bya-ma (bya ma); ༡༤ nag-pa (nag pa), byau (bya'u) (with Spica); ༡༥ sa-ri (sa ri); ༡༦ sa-ga (sa ga); ༡༧ lha-mtsams (lha mtshams), lag-sor (lag sor); ༡༨ snron (snron), ldeu (lde'u) (with Antares); ༡༩ snrubs (snrubs), sog-pa (sog pa): ༢༠ ču-stod (chu stod); ༢༡ ču-smad (chu smad), p̥ul (phul); ༢༢ gro-bžin (gro bzhin) and byi-bžin (byi bzhin) (considered as one constellation); ༢༣ mon-gre (mon gre), mon-dre (mon dre); ༢༤ mon-gru (mon gru), sgrog (sgrog); ༢༥ krums-stod (khrums stod); ༢༦ krums-smad (khrums smad); ༢༧ nam-gru (nam gru), še-sa (she sa).

pf. brgyugs (brgyugs), fut. brgyug (brgyug) 1. to run, frq.; to make haste, to hurry, to rush, sgor (sgor) to the door (out of the room) Dzl. ... kyi t̥óg-tu (kyi thog tu) upon ... Dzl.; *há-la gyug* (*ha la rgyug*) be off! get you gone! C. 1. to start (of a rail-way train) W.; rta-rgyúg-pa (rta rgyug pa) to ride full speed, to gallop; also sbst. race Glr. — 2. to go, to pass, to circulate, to be current; to be valid, to have force.
Lex.; Sch.: limit, term, aim, end; W.: task, lesson.
pride, ambition Sch.; grief, sorrow Schr. (?).
the nerves, sinews Sch.; cf. brgyúṅs-pa (brgyungs pa).

1. Ssk. तन्तु, तन्त्री string, cord, but only in certain relations: the string of a bow; rgya-rgyúd (rgya rgyud) Chinese string Mil.; string, chord, of a musical instrument, rgyud-máṅs (rgyud mangs) harp; chain, v. lu-gu (lu gu); mostly fig.: ri-rgyúd (ri rgyud), gaṅs-rgyúd (gangs rgyud) chain of mountains, ridge of snowy hills; also thread of tradition, i.e. continuous, uninterrupted tradition, so in: ka-rgyúd (ka rgyud), dgoṅs-rgyúd (dgongs rgyud), čos-rgyúd (chos rgyud), bka-rgyúd (bka' rgyud) (v. bka (bka')), sńyan-rgyúd (snyan rgyud) = bka-rgyúd (bka' rgyud), frq. in Mil.; ytam-rgyúd (gtam rgyud) Zam. legendary tradition. — If used
